requirement: Stable schema version required before React or other consumers read dashboard data.
Generation time must identify when the read-only snapshot was produced.
Each generated snapshot must expose a producer-owned identity so browser refresh can detect valid changes without inventing state.
Each generated snapshot must expose a producer-owned content identity for read-only live refresh and last-known-good comparison.
Source files must identify existing TSV, Markdown, policy, and settings files using relative paths without raw logs, remote URLs, or unnecessary absolute paths.
Source commands must identify existing read-only commands used to build the snapshot without requiring prose parsing.
Non-fatal conditions must be visible without failing the whole snapshot.
Selected context must bind the active menu, workflow context, repository, current step, evidence, blockers, and next safe action before the UI presents context-specific live state.
Selected context menu id must come from producer-owned menu or workflow state rather than browser inference.
Selected context workflow context must be passed to product authority, Git, CI, Security, and evidence readers without fixed product-improvement assumptions.
Selected context target repository must identify the current repository scope without making missing product repositories block lesson-only progress.
Selected context target repository name must be safe display text derived from existing settings or product authority.
Selected context repository path state must avoid leaking unnecessary absolute paths while distinguishing missing product repositories.
Selected context product type must come from product manifests or policy data rather than UI stack-specific branches.
Selected context current step id must remain a producer-owned diagnostic key and not replace learner-facing display labels.
Selected context current step label must be producer-owned display data so the UI does not invent step labels from screenshots.
Selected context current step index must be producer-owned for current-step summaries and progress strips.
Selected context current step total must be producer-owned for current-step summaries and progress strips.
Selected context update time must be producer-owned so page headers can show freshness without browser inference.
Selected context Git status must be read from existing evidence or settings and must not cause dashboard-data to run Git.
Selected context CI status must be read from existing evidence and must not cause dashboard-data or the browser to query live CI.
Selected context Security status must be read from policy or evidence and must preserve approval and blocker boundaries.
Selected context evidence status must preserve not-run and stale states instead of collapsing them into unknown.
Selected context next safe action must be producer-owned display data and must not execute commands.
Selected context blockers must contain current-context blockers and stay separate from optional manual follow-ups.
Each displayed menu context must have complete producer-owned context data so the browser never switches to an invented or thin context.
Each contexts_by_menu entry must use the same structure and validation rules as selected_context.
The context map key and the entry menu id must identify the same selectable menu context.
Context map workflow context must be producer-owned for every selectable menu.
Context map repository data must be complete enough for page headers, menu strips, and Safety scope display.
Context map current step id must come from lesson state, workflow context map, or workflow evidence.
Context map Git status must be producer-owned or explicitly manual_required/not_run/stale.
Context map CI status must be producer-owned or explicitly manual_required/not_run/stale.
Context map Security status must be producer-owned or explicitly manual_required/not_run/stale.
Context map evidence status must preserve blocked, stale, not_run, and manual_required states.
Context map blockers must be scoped to the current menu and must not mix lesson-only progress with product-operation blockers unless the context requires product operations.
Available contexts must list menu choices the browser may display without inventing workflow or repository state.
Available context menu ids must use the same vocabulary as selected_context.menu_id.
Available context workflow contexts must let the UI switch views without hard-coded product context branches.
Available context target repository names must be safe display text and may be not applicable for lesson-repository contexts.
Available context status must summarize whether the context can be inspected without treating optional or not-run evidence as healthy.
Each partial failure must identify the unavailable or optional source.
Each partial failure must use the fixed status vocabulary subset for true failed, blocked, or unknown failure conditions.
Each partial failure must explain why the source was unavailable without raw logs or secrets.
Each partial failure may identify the manual follow-up command needed for authoritative validation.
The home summary must classify the current control-center context without making it a new source of truth.
The snapshot must explain the next safe action without executing it.
The overview primary action must be structured so the UI can render a compact next action without parsing prose.
The overview primary action must include a concise explanation for the suggested review action.
The overview primary action must identify the target surface or workflow without executing anything.
The overview primary action must identify the expected safe result in display-only text.
The overview primary action must expose risk as structured data.
The overview primary action must expose state as structured data.
The overview primary action must identify the source field or decision path that produced it.
Overview metric must be producer-owned so the browser does not invent overview progress.
Lesson metric must be producer-owned so health cards and Explore Pages do not hard-code lesson progress.
Workflow metric must be producer-owned so health cards and Explore Pages do not hard-code workflow progress.
Maintenance metric must be producer-owned so health cards and Explore Pages do not hard-code maintenance progress.
Security metric must be producer-owned so health cards and Explore Pages do not hard-code security progress.
Category metrics must include a total count.
Category metrics must include a healthy count.
Category metrics must include a warning count.
Category metrics must include a problem count.
Category metrics must include a producer-owned percentage for progress rings and Explore Pages.
Category metrics must include a unit label such as items, steps, or checks.
Category metrics must expose state as structured data.
Each concise guidance item must identify whether it belongs to the lesson surface or workflow surface.
Guidance must distinguish plain-language learner help from practical engineering workflow advice where needed.
Guidance must classify whether the point is informational, a caution, or a suggested next action.
Guidance messages must stay concise and safe for display without raw logs, secrets, or external payloads.
Guidance may point to an existing dashboard or check command without executing it.
Each blocker must identify the required source or gate that prevents safe progress.
Required blockers must stay separate from optional partial failures.
Each blocker must explain the condition without raw logs or secrets.
Each blocker may identify the manual follow-up command needed to unblock progress.
Each manual follow-up must identify an optional, cached, or unknown check without classifying it as a true partial failure.
Manual follow-ups must use a fixed non-authoritative status vocabulary.
Each manual follow-up must explain the check without raw logs or secrets.
Each manual follow-up may identify the manual command needed for authoritative validation.
STEP 1-7 data must come from existing lesson state and settings sources.
STEP 1-7 concise lesson points must come from structured lesson state/settings, not CLI prose.
STEP 1-7 lesson warnings must come from structured missing/unknown state, not CLI prose.
STEP 1-7 next learning action must be a read-only explanation derived from structured lesson state/settings.
STEP 1-14 data must come from existing lesson14 state and settings sources.
STEP 1-14 concise lesson points must come from structured lesson state/settings, not CLI prose.
STEP 1-14 lesson warnings must come from structured missing/unknown state, not CLI prose.
STEP 1-14 next learning action must be a read-only explanation derived from structured lesson state/settings.
Applied lesson data must reuse existing menu prerequisite and team-development status sources.
Applied lesson concise points must come from structured menu prerequisite state, not CLI prose.
Applied lesson warnings must come from structured missing/approval state, not CLI prose.
Applied lesson next learning action must be a read-only explanation derived from structured menu prerequisite state.
Product repository state must come from existing repository-boundary and product workflow sources.
Product document state must come from existing document and workflow-pair checks.
Documents page data must be producer-owned so React does not keep a fixed document array.
Documents page status must summarize the producer-owned documents catalog without browser inference.
Documents page groups must identify learner intent and display order from dashboard data.
Each document group must expose a stable id.
Each document group must expose a localization key for the primary label.
Each document group must expose a localization key for the group explanation.
Each document group must expose producer-owned display order.
Documents page catalog must list document cards from producer-owned data.
Each document catalog item must expose a stable id.
Each document catalog item must reference a known documents group.
Each document catalog item must expose a stable document role.
Each document catalog item must expose a safe relative path.
Each document catalog item must expose structured readiness or review state.
Each document catalog item must identify which producer source determined its status.
Each document catalog item must classify its intended audience without hard-coded UI branches.
Each document catalog item must expose producer-owned display order.
Each document catalog item must route related operational checks to an existing dashboard page.
Documents page must expose read-only reference commands for existing document guidance tools without browser execution.
Each related document command must expose a stable id.
Each related document command must expose a localization key for the primary label.
Each related document command must expose a localization key for the description.
Each related document command must expose a safe display-only command string.
Each related document command must expose producer-owned display order.
Product Git sync state must remain separate from Git policy readiness.
Product CI state must be optional or evidence-backed unless a required check is requested.
Git operation rows must describe Push, PR, CI, sync, and Merge settings from producer-owned workflow data.
Each Git operation row must expose a stable id for reusable rendering.
Each Git operation row must expose a display label without relying on one screenshot language.
Each Git operation row must expose structured status.
Each Git operation row must expose the configured mode or gate label as safe text.
Each Git operation row must describe what the operation state means without executing the operation.
Recent workflow execution rows must be producer-owned instead of being inferred from Git operation settings.
Each recent workflow row must expose a stable id.
Each recent workflow row must expose producer-owned time or freshness text.
Each recent workflow row must expose the workflow event type.
Each recent workflow row must expose target repository, branch, or PR scope without secrets.
Each recent workflow row must expose a concise event description.
Each recent workflow row must expose structured status.
Each recent workflow row may expose a display-only reference such as run, commit, or PR id.
Product authority must summarize external product repository structure, manifest, evidence, and product-operation blockers without UI-invented judgment.
Product authority status must preserve not-run and stale evidence states.
Product repository status must identify the configured product repository without blocking lesson-only progress.
Product repository status must expose the configured product repository name in safe text.
Product repository blockers must be scoped to product operations rather than lesson-only progress.
Product summary must expose the learner-confirmed product display profile from the external repository so UI does not infer names from repository paths or prose.
Product summary status must expose whether ops/PRODUCT_PROFILE.json is present, valid, and displayable without inferring a fallback name.
Product summary name must come from ops/PRODUCT_PROFILE.json display_name, using the producer fallback order only when the UI has not selected a locale.
Product summary display_name must expose locale-keyed product names declared by the product display profile.
Product summary Japanese display name must preserve the learner-confirmed Japanese artifact name when available.
Product summary English display name must preserve the learner-confirmed English artifact name when available.
Product summary description may expose short locale-keyed profile descriptions without requiring the UI to parse product documents.
Product summary source_documents must list only safe relative documents that informed the recommendation or confirmation.
Product summary source path must identify ops/PRODUCT_PROFILE.json as the canonical external repository profile.
Product repository file structure and roles must come from the external product repository's dedicated read-only index, not from dashboard-side filesystem scanning.
Product repository index status must show whether the external read-only index was available without becoming a product-operation gate blocker.
Product repository index path must identify the external repository-owned index file such as ops/REPOSITORY_INDEX.json.
Product repository index summary must expose producer-owned directory, file, and total counts for the external repository index.

Product repository index entries must include all non-hidden external repository directories and files except generated dependency directories; index-to-filesystem consistency belongs to the external repository main CI.
Product repository index roles must be supplied by the external repository so dashboard UI does not infer file responsibilities from paths.
Product authority must expose manifest coverage derived from product structure and ops manifests.
Missing required manifest or structure rows must be visible as source ids.
Missing optional manifest or structure rows must be visible without becoming blockers.
Product authority must expose latest evidence status and freshness for Git, docs, CI, security, approvals, and gates.
Each evidence summary item must use a stable source id.
Each evidence summary item must preserve evidence status vocabulary.
Each evidence summary item must expose freshness separately from status.
Each evidence summary item must expose authority separately from status.
Product-operation blockers must stay separate from lesson-only summary blockers.
Each product-operation blocker must identify the product authority source id.
Each product-operation blocker must preserve source status without collapsing not-run or stale states.
Product-operation blockers may expose read-only command previews for manual follow-up.
Maintenance sync state must come from existing as-built sync checks.
Workflow pair state must come from existing TASK_TRACKER/HANDOFF pair checks.
Developer memory state must come from existing document routes and checks.
Repo-local skill state must come from existing skill and AGENTS checks.
Maintenance evidence rows must expose synchronization and policy evidence as producer-owned rows.
Each maintenance evidence row must expose a stable id.
Each maintenance evidence row must expose a display label.
Each maintenance evidence row must identify whether the row is required or optional display evidence.
Each maintenance evidence row must expose structured status.
Each maintenance evidence row may reference a relative path or display-only command.
Git policy file/readiness must remain separate from gate passage.
Git workflow settings readiness must remain separate from policy readiness and approval.
Git workflow gate results must not be inferred from policy readiness.
Git operation approval must remain separate from policy and gate state.
Security policy readiness must remain separate from product gate passage.
Product-security gate results must not be inferred from policy readiness.
Dangerous operations must require explicit approval before execution is considered.
Security approval rows must expose approval state without creating a browser approval path.
Each Security approval row must expose a stable id.
Each Security approval row must expose a display label.
Each Security approval row must expose structured status.
Each Security approval row must explain the approval meaning as display-only data.
Each Security approval row may expose producer-owned freshness.
Dangerous operation rows must expose risky operation summaries without adding executable affordances.
Each dangerous operation row must expose a stable id.
Each dangerous operation row must expose a display label.
Each dangerous operation row must expose structured status.
Each dangerous operation row must explain why it is safe, blocked, or approval-gated.
Each dangerous operation row may expose producer-owned freshness.
Command previews must explain the intended outcome.
Command previews must identify the target repository, file, workflow, or service without unnecessary sensitive detail.
Command previews must classify risk before any future execution pathway is considered.
Command previews must explicitly identify whether developer approval is required.
Command previews for risky operations must identify the approval gate or state unknown.
Command previews should preserve command tokens without shell interpretation when possible.
Command previews may show manual command text but must not execute it.
The initial dashboard must remain preview_only for dangerous operations.
Initial dashboard command previews must be explicitly non-executable.
Command preview groups must let Safety pages group display-only previews without inferring categories in React.
Each command preview group must expose a stable id.
Each command preview group must expose a display label.
Each command preview group must expose a risk level.
Each command preview group must expose a count derived by the producer.
